bugs.criteria_violated: Agent does not ask for the same information twice
bugs.timestamp_approx: after initial name/DOB collection, when agent asks "If you're not sure, I can confirm your name and date of birth again. Which would you prefer?"
bugs.what_happened: Agent redundantly asked the patient to reconfirm name and date of birth that had already been provided and confirmed.
bugs.what_should_have_happened: Agent should have proceeded with verification using the already‑provided information without asking for it again.

bugs.criteria_violated: Agent does not abruptly end the conversation without resolution
bugs.timestamp_approx: final lines, after saying "Hello. You've reached the Pretty Good AI test line. Goodbye."
bugs.what_happened: Agent ended the call abruptly without addressing the patient’s insurance question or providing next steps.
bugs.what_should_have_happened: Agent should have either completed the verification, transferred to a human representative with a clear handoff, or offered a callback while ensuring the patient’s query was resolved.

bugs.criteria_violated: Agent confirms whether BCBS PPO is accepted
bugs.timestamp_approx: throughout the call, especially after verification attempts
bugs.what_happened: Agent never confirmed whether Blue Cross Blue Shield PPO is accepted, stating instead that it was unable to verify information.
bugs.what_should_have_happened: Agent should have either confirmed acceptance of BCBS PPO based on available data or clearly communicated the lack of coverage and next steps.

bugs.criteria_violated: Agent provides any relevant copay or coverage information
bugs.timestamp_approx: end of interaction when patient asked about insurance acceptance
bugs.what_happened: Agent did not provide any copay or coverage details related to BCBS PPO.
bugs.what_should_have_happened: If the insurance is accepted, the agent should have supplied typical copay amounts or directed the patient to where that information can be found.

bugs.criteria_violated: Agent offers to help schedule if insurance is accepted
bugs.what_happened: Agent never offered to assist with scheduling an appointment even if the insurance were accepted.
bugs.what_should_have_happened: Agent should have offered to schedule an appointment or set up a callback once insurance coverage was confirmed.

global_criteria_results.criterion: Agent verifies patient identity before taking any action
global_criteria_results.note: Identity verification was initiated before attempting to answer the insurance question.

global_criteria_results.criterion: Agent never confirms an action without collecting required information first
Agent does not get stuck in a loop or repeat itself
Agent handles silence and pauses gracefully
Agent does not ask for the same information twice
global_criteria_results.note: Repeated request to reconfirm name and DOB.

global_criteria_results.criterion: Agent does not confirm things it cannot do (e.g. Sunday appointments)
Agent does not make up information about hours, insurance, or doctors
Agent remains polite even with difficult or confused patients
Agent does not abruptly end the conversation without resolution
global_criteria_results.note: Call ended abruptly with no resolution to the insurance inquiry.

scenario_criteria_results.criterion: Agent confirms whether BCBS PPO is accepted
scenario_criteria_results.note: Never provided a confirmation; said verification could not be done.

scenario_criteria_results.criterion: Agent provides any relevant copay or coverage information
scenario_criteria_results.note: No copay or coverage details were given.

scenario_criteria_results.criterion: Agent offers to help schedule if insurance is accepted

summary: The agent failed to meet several critical criteria, notably repeating already‑provided information, ending the call abruptly without resolving the insurance query, and not confirming BCBS PPO acceptance or providing related details. These failures lead to an overall unsuccessful call handling.
